title: Elin Hilderbrand talks ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ Nantucket, and her next book
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Elin Hilderbrand discusses 'The Five-Star Weekend' TV adaptation on Peacock, starring Jennifer Garner.
Filming took place on Nantucket, reflecting Hilderbrand's deep connection to the island.
Hilderbrand continues writing in retirement, with new projects in development including a sequel to 'The Academy'.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (July 15)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington saw various home sales, including a 3,540 sqft Colonial on Old Farm Road sold for $1,225,000.
In Acton, a 5,081 sqft Colonial on Woodchester Drive was sold for $2,170,000.
Arlington reported a Colonial-style home on Churchill Avenue selling for $1,980,000.

title: Active-duty Everett firefighter dies from occupational cancer after 30 years of service, department says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Everett firefighter John Benoit, 63, died from occupational cancer after over 30 years of service.
John Benoit, who joined the Everett Fire Department in 1996, was a former Air Force enlistee.
Benoit's family requests donations to the 100 Club of Massachusetts instead of flowers in his memory.

title: 2 Dorchester women charged in alleged human trafficking scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Yuberkys Recci and Keysha Ortiz were arrested in Dorchester for allegedly operating a human trafficking scheme.
The victim, a homeless woman, was coerced into sex work and transported across Massachusetts and New Hampshire.
The women face multiple charges, including trafficking, kidnapping, drug distribution, and weapons offenses, and are held without bail.

title: ‘Brilliant, unique soul’: Mother remembers Fall River teen who drowned in pond
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: 17-year-old Tristan Major, also known as Rohan Rhys, drowned at South Watuppa Pond in Fall River on July 12.
Mother Kailyn Lord described her son as a 'brilliant, unique soul' in a GoFundMe post, seeking memorial funding.
B.M.C. Durfee High School provided counselors after the tragedy; police found no foul play in the incident.

title: Jennifer Garner posts throwback video of karaoke night at Nantucket restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Garner shared a throwback video of a karaoke night at Nantucket's Gaslight with her 'The Five-Star Weekend' co-stars.
Local guide Bill Jamieson joined Garner and castmates onstage, performing ABBA's 'Dancing Queen' on September 30, 2025.
Garner praised Nantucket's attractions and mentioned visiting the Whaling Museum three times while filming the TV show there.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell and Heather Alterisio
bullets: Canadian wildfire smoke causes yellow skies and moderate air quality in Boston, affecting sensitive groups.
Another smoke plume is expected Wednesday, bringing hazy skies and limiting temperatures in Boston.
Meteorologists advise precautions as high temperatures and wildfire smoke persist in Boston area.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A special traffic cone signed by Glasgow officials arrived in Boston to symbolize the new sister city partnership.
The cone, featuring city-themed illustrations, will tour Boston to raise funds for mental health resources.
Boston and Glasgow's partnership, inspired by 2026 FIFA World Cup connections, formally begins next year.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud and falsifying Red Line track inspections.
Seven total defendants, including four previously indicted, face conspiracy charges in new federal case.
Employees allegedly worked on personal vehicles during shifts and submitted false overtime hours.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France's quarterfinal victory marked the conclusion of the World Cup games held at Foxborough's Gillette Stadium.
Boston's World Cup involvement ended positively, garnering top rankings among U.S. venues and fostering international connections.
The high cost of the World Cup highlights challenges of a potential Olympic bid, projecting expenditures far exceeding $100 million.
